raw_text: > javier moliner teruel +34 679638315 javier.molinerq gmail.com mí javierimt 8) birth date: 05/november/1986 mechanical engineer with experience in design within the aerospace and elevation industries, having worked and lived in the uk (london) and france (paris). innovation passionate. i consider myself a problem solver, creative, sociable and analytical. work experience. international projects engineer at otis. madrid (04/2015-present) - project manager for international projects, working closely with our workforce on target countries to ensure a successful deployment of our products and brand. - interact with international otis branches, gathering all the necessary information to ensure a correct study of the projects and clients satisfaction. - evaluate the technical feasibility of the modifications that need to be done to standard elevators to comply with clients special needs. - product definition and design to carry out validated modifications. aeronautical design engineer at dassault aviation. paris. (06/2012- 04/2015) project developed as part of the aeronautical systems engineering team, at dassault aviation in paris using catia v6. design of the oxygen (ata35) and water&waste (ata38) systems for the dassault falcon 5x (f5x), from a preliminary design phase to a production phase. main tasks were: - technologies choice for both systems. finding the best technological solutions to fulfill all the requirements each system had. - parts design, designing special solutions for special problems. - secondary structure design proposals, working closely with the structure design team. - product lifecycle management. managing the different phases the project passed, working with engineering change authorizations and related documentation, acquiring extensive experience in that field. - dmu rigid and flexible tubes routing. design engineer at ausy engineering. getafe. (09/2011 06/2012) activities undertaken at ausy were focused on the development of the airbus a350xwb: 3d design with catia v5 of machined parts and sheet metal parts within projects regarding the 19.1 section of the a350xwb and the lightening of the belly fairing. parts design in composite materials. catia v5. currículum vitae: javier moliner teruel - documentation of structural parts, assemblies and installations within the tail section of the a350x wb. e design engineer at the superior council of scientific research: csic. 2010 design of a dish / stirling solar energy plant using solidworks and catia v5 for the state project called cenit consollda. languages. e english. professional competence. 1 year stay in london (uk) working and studying between 2010 and 2011. e french. professional competence. 3 year working stay in paris and a 10 months stay in tarbes (france) while studying at the paul sabatier iii - toulouse university. e spanish. native. academic training. e 2010. toulouse university paul sabatier iii. licence profesionnelle icp: ingénierie de conception et prototypage. competences acquired: industrial design cad: catia v5, proengineer, solidworks, autocad. product design: from conception to fabrication. e 2010. university of zaragoza (spain). bachelor of engineering: mechanical engineering. competences acquired: mechanical design, structural design, thermodynamics, fluid dynamics, production methods. e 2012. dassault systemes. training: dassault aviation - cellule designer. training content: methodological complements, management of catalogues, life cycle management, catia context management and document management. other work experience. e parts design for classic cars, designing solutions for those rare pieces that can no longer be found on the market. e active participation on classic cars restoration. e 2011. w hotel london. customer service and logistic duties for the spa of the w hotel london. 5 stars hotel. other issues. e willing to travel. e 3d printing and photography passionate. e as an automotive industry enthusiast, finalist of the contest seat: engineering and innovation within the context of the xii international congress of project engineering. page 2 +34 679638315. javier.moliner@ gmail.com
